name: context-engineering
description: Foundation in context engineering for building, optimizing, and debugging production-grade AI agent systems. Covers context curation, harness engineering, multi-agent architectures, and memory systems. Activates when the agent system exhibits context degradation (lost-in-the-middle, tool bloat, overflow), skill boundary confusion, cross-session amnesia, or multi-agent inefficiency. Designed to integrate with and enhance the existing 55+ marketing and business intelligence skills ecosystem.
author: muratcankoylan/Agent-Skills-for-Context-Engineering

skills.name: context-fundamentals
skills.description: Establish mental models for what context is, how attention mechanics work, why context quality > quantity, and the anatomy of a context window. Activates when planning agent architecture or explaining how context components affect model behavior.

skills.name: context-degradation
skills.description: Diagnose and mitigate five predictable failure patterns: lost-in-middle, poisoning, distraction, confusion, and clash. Activates when diagnosing attention failures, context poisoning, or degraded agent performance across long sessions.

skills.name: context-compression
skills.description: Design compression strategies for long-running sessions. Anchored Iterative, Opaque, and Regenerative approaches. Activates when preserving useful state while reducing conversation, tool-output, or trajectory size under context pressure.

skills.name: context-optimization
skills.description: Apply compaction, masking, and caching strategies to extend effective context capacity. Activates when improving token efficiency, retrieval precision, prefix reuse, masking, partitioning, or budget allocation.

skills.name: multi-agent-patterns
skills.description: Orchestrator, peer-to-peer, and hierarchical multi-agent architectures. Core insight: sub-agents exist primarily to isolate context. Activates when choosing coordination patterns, isolating context across agents, or designing handoffs.

skills.name: memory-systems
skills.description: Short-term, long-term, and graph-based memory architectures. Framework comparison (Mem0, Zep/Graphiti, Letta, Cognee, LangMem). Activates when persisting cross-session knowledge, tracking entities over time, or designing retrieval and update semantics.

skills.name: tool-design
skills.description: Agent-tool contract design, tool surface consolidation, description engineering, and error message optimization. Activates when defining agent-tool contracts, consolidating tool surfaces, or making tool errors actionable.

skills.name: filesystem-context
skills.description: Filesystem-based dynamic context discovery, tool output offloading, plan persistence, and scratchpad patterns. Activates when moving large or durable context into files, creating scratchpads, or coordinating agents through shared artifacts.

skills.name: evaluation
skills.description: Deterministic checks, multi-dimensional rubrics, and outcome measurement frameworks for agent systems. Activates when creating deterministic checks, rubrics, regression suites, production monitoring, or quality gates.

skills.name: advanced-evaluation
skills.description: LLM-as-a-Judge techniques: direct scoring, pairwise comparison, rubric generation, and bias mitigation. Activates when using LLM judges, pairwise comparison, calibration, or human-aligned quality assessment.

skills.name: harness-engineering
skills.description: Autonomous agent harnesses with locked metrics, durable logs, novelty gates, rollback, and human approval boundaries. Activates when designing autonomous loops with locked evaluators, editable surfaces, and governance.

skills.name: project-development
skills.description: Task-model fit analysis, batch pipeline architecture, structured output design, and cost estimation for LLM projects. Activates when deciding whether an LLM is appropriate, shaping batch pipelines, or creating staged artifacts.

skills.name: latent-briefing
skills.description: Share task-relevant orchestrator state with workers via task-guided KV cache compaction. Activates when sharing orchestrator trajectory with workers when models are compatible.

whenNotToActivate: For pure copywriting, cold outreach, or content strategy — use existing marketing skills (copywriting, cold-email, content-strategy)
For brand strategy, positioning, or pricing — use product-marketing or pricing skills
For SEO audit, A/B testing, or analytics setup — use seo-audit, ab-testing, or analytics skills
This skill is a foundational layer, not a replacement for your 55+ domain-specific marketing skills
